title: Quiz: Introduction to Solid State Physics
description: Test your understanding of solid state physics fundamentals

questions.question: How many Bravais lattices exist in three dimensions?
questions.explanation: There are 14 Bravais lattices in 3D, distributed among 7 crystal systems. These represent all distinct ways to fill 3D space with a periodic lattice.

questions.question: What does Bloch's theorem state about electron wavefunctions in a periodic potential?

options.text: Wavefunctions are plane waves modulated by a periodic function

questions.explanation: Bloch's theorem states that ψ_nk(r) = e^(ik·r) u_nk(r), where u_nk(r) has the periodicity of the lattice. The wavefunction is a plane wave modulated by a periodic part.

questions.question: What distinguishes a semiconductor from an insulator?

options.text: Semiconductors have a small band gap (0.1-3 eV)

questions.explanation: Semiconductors have small band gaps (typically 0.1-3 eV) allowing thermal excitation of carriers. Insulators have large gaps (>3 eV) preventing significant carrier population at room temperature.

questions.question: What is the first Brillouin zone?

options.text: The unit cell in real space
The Wigner-Seitz cell of the reciprocal lattice
The gap between valence and conduction bands

questions.explanation: The first Brillouin zone is the Wigner-Seitz cell of the reciprocal lattice. It's the primitive cell in k-space that contains all unique k-points before periodicity in the reciprocal lattice.

questions.question: Metals require denser k-point grids than insulators for accurate calculations.
questions.explanation: True. Metals have a Fermi surface that must be sampled accurately, requiring dense k-grids. Insulators have smooth, gapped bands that converge with fewer k-points.

questions.question: Which are FCC crystal structures? (Select all)
questions.explanation: Cu and Al crystallize in FCC structures. Fe is BCC at room temperature (α-iron), and Na is also BCC. FCC metals typically include Cu, Ag, Au, Al, Ni, and Pt.
